title: East Anglia: Duxford, Long Melford & Lavenham

main_route.excerpt: We reached Duxford & looked at aircraft before & after lunch.
We camped behind the 3 Bottles at Great Yeldan, a tilted field in which Simon nearly got the van stuck.
Long Melford has 5 stars in the church book, so we drove there & washed up while waiting for the service. It is a splendid church.
We drove on to Lavenham (4 star church) which we didn't like as much, though it a purer style of arch+, perpendicular. We had lunch on the road outside.

boundary_flags: mixed_start: B_0084_R begins mid-sentence finishing a previous trip; Saturday October 8th heading marks the start of this trip
summary: A short autumn weekend trip from Birmingham to East Anglia. On Saturday (Oct 8) Simon and Eve visited the Imperial War Museum at Duxford and overnighted at the 3 Bottles pub campsite in Great Yeldham, Essex. On Sunday (Oct 9) they explored Long Melford and Lavenham, admiring both villages' notable medieval wool churches and Lavenham's Guildhall, before returning home.
extraction_notes: Only Simon and Eve are named as travellers (Thomas is noted as about to go to Russia, John is working in Dudley). 'Great Yeldan' is almost certainly Great Yeldham, Essex — a village with a pub called the Three Bottles. The travellers tried to visit 'Laurel & Dick Spooner' (unidentified friends) but they were out; no stop was made. Long Melford and Lavenham are visited on Sunday as route stops on what appears to be the homeward leg (no return to the campsite mentioned). The mixed_start boundary means B_0084_R opens with the end of the previous trip (ferry arrival, drive home, Moseley balti).
